title: [InstCombine] Use m_Power2 to check for powers of 2

tests.source_program:
define void @alloca_alignment_check() {
  %a = alloca i8, align 32
  %p = ptrtoint i8* %a to i64
  %rem = urem i64 %p, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %rem, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %ok, label %bad
ok:
  ret void
bad:
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions allocation removal with alignment checking but no known tests cover alloca alignment verification patterns that use m_Power2 for alignment validation

alive2.src:
define void @alloca_alignment_check() {
  %a = alloca i8, align 32
  %p = ptrtoint i8* %a to i64
  %rem = urem i64 %p, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %rem, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %ok, label %bad
ok:
  ret void
bad:
  ret void
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define void @alloca_alignment_check() {
  br i1 true, label %ok, label %bad

ok:                                               ; preds = %0
  ret void

bad:                                              ; preds = %0
  ret void
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define void @alloca_alignment_check() {
#0:
  %a = alloca i64 1, align 32
  %p = ptrtoint ptr %a to i64
  %rem = urem i64 %p, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %rem, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %ok, label %bad

bad:
  ret void

ok:
  ret void
}
=>
define void @alloca_alignment_check() {
#0:
  br i1 1, label %ok, label %bad

bad:
  ret void

ok:
  ret void
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes vector cases with i16/i32 elements; this tests scalar i32 shift pattern using m_Power2 matcher in InstCombine

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @scalar_shl_lshr_pow2_i32(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %shl = shl i32 %x, 4
  %lshr = lshr i32 %shl, 4
  %r = and i32 %lshr, 15
  ret i32 %r
}
=>
define i32 @scalar_shl_lshr_pow2_i32(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %r = and i32 %x, 15
  ret i32 %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes vector cases and InstCombine transforms, but scalar constant expression getExactLogBase2 simplification via m_Power2 is not demonstrated in existing tests. This tests the Constants.cpp change for scalar integers.
